commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an OpenAI-compatible generic embedding provider to the existing OpenAI plugin, bridges explicit memory-core and gateway embedding configs to generic embedding providers, updates SDK/docs/changelog surfaces, and adds focused provider, bridge, and gateway tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The PR body includes after-patch local Ollama, provider-adapter, memory-bridge, and gateway coverage evidence for the new behavior.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer API/ownership and launch-proof judgment, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: feat(openai): add generic OpenAI-compatible embeddings
target.body:
## Summary

This replaces Mariano Belinky's stacked embedding follow-ups with one draft PR to `main` that keeps the OpenAI-compatible provider inside the existing OpenAI plugin.

- Brings in Mariano's memory-core bridge from #84991 so explicit memory embedding configs can consume generic `embeddingProviders`.
- Adds Mariano's OpenAI-compatible generic embedding provider behavior from #84998, but registers it from `extensions/openai` instead of adding a new `extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings` package.
- Treats `api.registerEmbeddingProvider` as the single registration point for the new `openai-compatible` embedding capability. The existing memory-specific API remains compatibility for existing memory adapters only; this provider is not also registered there.
- Routes both memory-core and the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` gateway endpoint through the generic provider registry for explicit provider configs.
- Declares `contracts.embeddingProviders: ["openai-compatible"]` on the existing OpenAI plugin and registers `api.registerEmbeddingProvider(...)` from the OpenAI plugin entry.
- Keeps memory-specific providers winning first and keeps generic providers out of memory auto-selection.
- Preserves env SecretRef and env-template API key resolution for `agents.*.memorySearch.remote.apiKey`; unresolved env-template refs now fail instead of being sent as literal bearer tokens.
- Preserves configured provider aliases for OpenAI-compatible model-provider entries, so `memorySearch.provider` can point at a custom `models.providers.<id>` with `api: "openai-completions"`, `api: "openai-responses"`, or a baseUrl-only OpenAI-compatible config and reuse its base URL, API key, headers, and active model prefix stripping.
- Keeps memory-lancedb documentation on that plugin's existing direct OpenAI-compatible embedding path instead of claiming it can load generic-only providers.
- Updates the scratch implementation plan with the single generic-registration constraint, the gateway consumer requirement, and this replacement landing path.
- Defers Mariano's deprecation-only #85072 so `memoryEmbeddingProviders` remains compatibility without adding early deprecation warnings in this PR.

Credit: Soham Patankar / @yaanfpv receives explicit credit in this PR body and via a `Co-authored-by: Soham Patankar <102520430+yaanfpv@users.noreply.github.com>` trailer on the OpenAI-compatible provider implementation commit. Lineage starts with #80476 and #80479, continues through #84930 and #84947, and then through Mariano Belinky PRs #84991 and #84998. This PR preserves that chain while adapting the provider for the existing `extensions/openai` placement requested by maintainers.

## Verification

After rebasing onto current `upstream/main` (`b3ec11b052`), verified tree `f2e5f093e692` at prior head `519441e208`; metadata-only co-author trailer rewrite now at head `7bb851e2af`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts` -> passed, 7 files / 61 tests.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-openai-generic-embedding-provider.tsbuildinfo` -> passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-openai-generic-embedding-provider.tsbuildinfo` -> passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/embeddings-http.ts src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-providers.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts extensions/openai/index.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` -> passed.
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check` -> passed.
- `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check` -> passed.
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs` -> passed.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/adding-capabilities.md docs/plugins/manifest.md docs/plugins/memory-lancedb.md docs/plugins/plugin-inventory.md docs/plugins/reference.md docs/plugins/reference/openai.md docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md docs/reference/memory-config.md` -> passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` -> passed.
- Earlier raw local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint proof against already-installed `qwen2.5:3b` returned one 2048-dimensional embedding.
- Earlier new provider proof against local Ollama `qwen2.5:3b` returned query and batch embeddings with 2048-dimensional vectors.
- Earlier memory-core bridge proof against local Ollama `qwen2.5:3b` through the existing OpenAI plugin returned query and batch embeddings with 2048-dimensional vectors.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: memory-core and the gateway `/v1/embeddings` endpoint can consume a generic OpenAI-compatible embedding provider through the existing OpenAI plugin, without adding a separate OpenAI-compatible embeddings extension, without registering the new provider through a second memory-specific API path, and without making generic providers memory auto-selection candidates.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on `feat/openai-generic-embedding-provider`, with local Ollama 0.24.0 and already-installed model `qwen2.5:3b`. No model was downloaded for this proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebased onto current `upstream/main`; ran the focused Vitest, tsgo, oxlint, SDK, docs, and whitespace commands listed above; previously called raw local Ollama `POST http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1/embeddings` with model `qwen2.5:3b`; previously ran a `node --import tsx` script that used `openAICompatibleEmbeddingProviderAdapter.create(...)` against the same Ollama endpoint; previously ran a second `node --import tsx` script that registered the existing OpenAI plugin with `ownerPluginId: "openai"`, resolved `provider: "openai-compatible"` through memory-core `createEmbeddingProvider(...)`, and called `embedQuery` plus `embedBatch`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "rawOllama": {
    "ok": true,
    "count": 1,
    "dimensions": 2048,
    "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
    "error": null
  },
  "providerProof": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
    "queryDimensions": 2048,
    "batchCount": 2,
    "batchDimensions": [2048, 2048]
  },
  "memoryBridgeProof": {
    "registered": [{ "ownerPluginId": "openai", "id": "openai-compatible" }],
    "requestedProvider": "openai-compatible",
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
    "queryDimensions": 2048,
    "batchCount": 2,
    "batchDimensions": [2048, 2048]
  },
  "gatewayBridgeProof": {
    "explicitProvider": "openai-compatible",
    "aliasProvider": "ollama-local",
    "httpEndpoint": "/v1/embeddings",
    "coveredBy": "src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts"
  },
  "singleRegistrationCheck": {
    "newProvider": "openai-compatible",
    "registeredWith": "api.registerEmbeddingProvider",
    "notRegisteredWith": "api.registerMemoryEmbeddingProvider"
  },
  "aliasPrefixCheck": {
    "providerAlias": "ollama-local",
    "configuredModel": "ollama-local/qwen2.5:3b",
    "requestModel": "qwen2.5:3b"
  },
  "secretRefCheck": {
    "missingEnvTemplate": "throws unresolved SecretRef instead of sending literal template"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: explicit memory config for `provider: "openai-compatible"` resolves through the generic provider registry owned by the existing OpenAI plugin, posts OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` requests to local Ollama, preserves memory runtime metadata, returns real 2048-dimensional vectors, strips active custom provider prefixes before the request, rejects unresolved env-template API keys, routes gateway embeddings through the same generic registry for explicit providers, does not advertise structured memory input support for generic text-only providers, treats blank remote overrides as unset for configured aliases, and leaves memory `auto` selection unchanged.

What was not tested: external hosted OpenAI-compatible services beyond local Ollama were not called.
target.author: dutifulbob

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
